channel.title: The Git Reporter
channel.description: A code-native newspaper of meaningful software change.

item.title: An Agent's Sign-In Link Is Not a Timeout
item.description: OpenWork's newest MCP work carries a provider's structured authorization link through a failed tool call, so the person who owns the account can act instead of watching an agent retry the wrong problem.

item.title: API Discovery: One Reasoning-Effort Dial Across Provider Integrations
item.description: LangChain's new reasoning_effort interface lets a builder express an effort choice once across supported chat integrations—provided the selected model profile says that choice exists.

item.title: Builder Work: Pi Counts the Work Its Agent Hides
item.description: Pi's latest accounting patch treats compaction, branch summaries, and tool-result hooks as model work with receipts—not invisible overhead beneath an agent's final answer.

item.title: The Diff Can't Be Guesswork Once an Agent Writes
item.description: OpenClaw is making a simple rule explicit: when an agent writes a file, a review surface should show the patch it can prove—and show uncertainty when it cannot.

item.title: API Discovery: Tail a Delegated Hermes Agent at Work
item.description: Hermes' public delegate_task surface can now return a live, local log for each child—useful when a background fan-out needs observation without flooding the parent conversation.

item.title: Codex Stops Asking Its Subagents the Same Question Twice
item.description: A Codex TUI patch cuts duplicate status reads by treating new, resumed, and actively streaming agent threads as different lifecycle states.

item.title: A Computer-Use Agent Needs a Second Meaning of Success
item.description: Hermes now tells a desktop-driving agent whether an input merely reached a driver or was actually confirmed to have changed the interface—and it makes foreground escalation a separate decision.

item.title: API Discovery: A Scheduler Should Ask Which France It Is In
item.description: France’s public holiday API gives an agent zone-specific statutory dates—but only a person can decide whether that means a team is unavailable.

item.title: Gemini CLI’s Caretaker Has Permission to Read, Not to Act
item.description: Gemini CLI’s new issue-triage worker gives its model a read-only repository view, then keeps labels, comments, retries, and escalation in a separate validated program.

item.title: Builder Work: When an MCP Session Dies, Rebuild the Whole Registry
item.description: Crush’s agent-attributed MCP recovery work teaches a hard-earned rule: a connection is not back until every capability it advertises is back too.

item.title: The Hard Part of an Agent Protocol Rewrite Is Yesterday
item.description: Tau is replacing its legacy agent protocol with Pi-compatible shapes, but its more consequential promise sits at the JSONL boundary: old sessions are meant to cross without pretending the old runtime still exists.

item.title: API Discovery: Tau's Python Extension Seam
item.description: Tau's public Python extension interface gives a coding agent a place to turn tool completions into a small, inspectable deployment or review ledger.

item.title: Tau's Protocol Rewrite Keeps Yesterday's Sessions
item.description: Tau is choosing Pi-compatible extension and event shapes, but its public migration code makes a more human promise: a user's old session should have a route into the new system.

item.title: Codex Keeps the Prompt After You Interrupt It
item.description: A new Codex TUI change treats an interrupted request as part of the record, then gives the next instruction a clean composer.

item.title: Open Interpreter: roots, maintainers, and the contributor advantage
item.description: The new Rust Open Interpreter is openly building on Codex; its public advantage is upstream leverage, not a proven inside track.

item.title: Background Agent Work Needs a Receipt
item.description: Hermes and OpenClaw are fixing different failure windows in the same promise: delegated work has to arrive somewhere a person can use it.

item.title: API Discovery: Georisques Turns a Commune Into a Risk Checklist
item.description: Géorisques’ public commune endpoint can give an agent a responsible starting checklist—if it never mistakes the list for an address-level verdict.

item.title: Builder Work: Crush Builds Its Sidebar With Crush
item.description: Crush’s agent-attributed sidebar feature is a useful reminder: delegate the bounded interaction, then inspect the state machine—not just the screen.

item.title: Pi Stops Treating Session Affinity as One Header
item.description: Pi’s OpenRouter fix shows why “OpenAI-compatible” requests still need provider-specific routing details.

item.title: Codex Makes Thread History a Rebuildable View
item.description: Two fresh commits separate a coding agent’s durable JSONL record from its fast SQLite history view—and teach that view how to catch up after it falls behind.

item.title: Alec Radford's GPT Lineage in Code
item.description: The first two public GPT repositories record a team handoff and a changing release boundary—not a hero commit—from task fine-tuning toward tokenization, inference, and sampling while the training system stayed out of view.

item.title: Fabrice Bellard's Code Gems
item.description: QuickJS, TinyCC, early QEMU, and early FFmpeg reveal a repeatable engineering move: find an expensive handoff, collapse it on the common path, and leave a narrow boundary others can build around.

item.title: OpenWork Gives MCP Failures a Useful Address
item.description: A new diagnostics layer separates DNS, TLS, HTTP, OAuth, protocol, and provider failures, then returns a bounded reference and the person who can act—without logging the raw connection secret trail.

item.title: Shepherd Source Profile
item.description: The project's v0.3.0 source keeps a task's changes beside the working tree as a retained changeset, then makes settlement explicit and one-time.

item.title: Hermes Turns Health Checks Into a Runtime Contract
item.description: A new authenticated readiness endpoint checks the parts of an agent gateway that can make it unreliable—without reading payloads, leaking configuration, or trying to repair anything.

item.title: API Discovery: Let VigiEau Answer the Water-Rule Question
item.description: VigiEau’s public API turns a commune or coordinates into the current alert zone, restrictions, and underlying order—useful for a local service that needs to ask what rules apply here, today.

item.title: Builder Work: Saracroche Keeps the Whole Boundary in View
item.description: The transferable lesson in Saracroche’s AI-assisted iOS work is not “use less AI.” It is to trace every state transition across an extension boundary before delegating a local implementation.

item.title: How Saracroche carries nearly 17 million phone entries through one narrow iOS doorway
item.description: Saracroche turns 1,548 compact French call-list patterns into 16.956 million represented numbers, then hands them to CallKit 10,000 at a time. The source explains the design; it does not prove every entry succeeds on a phone.

item.title: OpenWork Puts the Whole Desktop in the Air-Gapped Box
item.description: OpenWork’s new organization installer bundles the generic installer, the standard desktop artifact, and organization configuration together—making an offline setup path a public product contract rather than a deployment footnote.

item.title: Entire Gives Agent Work a Separate Git Address
item.description: The CLI stores prompts, tool calls, touched files, and now images on a checkpoint branch—making an AI coding session resumable, and potentially shareable by accident.
